steps.title: Substitute x = 63 into the model
steps.content: The equation $y=0.75x+2.5$ predicts the number of larvae $y$ from the number of worker ants $x$. Since the colony has $63$ worker ants, plug in $x=63$: $$y=0.75(63)+2.5.$$

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• A) $50$ — matches $49.75$ rounded. Correct.
  • B) $66$ — may come from adding $63+2.5$ and rounding, but the model multiplies 63 by 0.75 first. Eliminated.
  • C) $81$ — this is roughly the worker count needed for 63 predicted larvae, not the predicted larvae for 63 workers. Eliminated.
  • D) $205$ — may come from adding $0.75+2.5$ and then multiplying by $63$, giving $204.75$. Eliminated.
A is correct.
